question: [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker calling buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with a valid targetBuyerAssets and a single fully-liquid sell offer trigger an OutOfOffers() revert because the rounding in TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits (mulDivUp for sell offers) produces a units value whose forward price computation yields filledBuyerAssets != targetFilledBuyerAssets by exactly 1 wei, violating the invariant that a valid bundle with sufficient offer liquidity must complete successfully? Preconditions: market exists, offer has enough remaining units, referralFeePct=0, targetBuyerAssets chosen near a rounding boundary of buyerPrice. Call sequence: deploy MidnightBundles, create market, sign sell offer, call buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with targetBuyerAssets=N. Invariant tested: exact-fill equality check on line 224 must not reject valid fills due to forward/inverse rounding asymmetry. Scoped impact: fund freeze — taker cannot execute a valid buy bundle. Proof idea: fuzz targetBuyerAssets in [1, 1e18] with a fixed tick and assert that for every value where sufficient offer units exist, the call does not revert with OutOfOffers().

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker calling supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget with a valid targetSellerAssets and a single fully-liquid buy offer trigger an OutOfOffers() revert because TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits (mulDivDown for buy offers) produces a units value whose forward sellerAssets computation yields filledSellerAssets != targetFilledSellerAssets by 1 wei, violating the invariant that a valid bundle with sufficient offer liquidity must complete? Preconditions: market exists, buy offer has enough remaining units, referralFeePct=0, targetSellerAssets chosen near a rounding boundary of sellerPrice. Call sequence: supply collateral, call supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget with targetSellerAssets=N. Invariant tested: exact-fill equality check on line 303 must not reject valid fills due to forward/inverse rounding asymmetry. Scoped impact: fund freeze — taker cannot execute a valid sell bundle; collateral already supplied is locked in Midnight for the taker. Proof idea: fuzz targetSellerAssets in [1, 1e18] with a fixed tick and assert no OutOfOffers() when offer has sufficient units.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged taker trigger an unconditional revert of buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral when any offer in the takes array has a tick whose computed buyerPrice exceeds WAD (i.e., tickToPrice returns a value such that buyerPrice = sellerPrice + settlementFee > 1e18), because ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits calls TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits which reverts with PriceGreaterThanOne() outside the try/catch block on line 208-213, blocking the entire bundle even though the offer would have been skipped by the inner try/catch? Preconditions: market near maturity with non-zero settlementFee, offer tick chosen such that tickToPrice(tick) + settlementFee > WAD. Call sequence: create market, advance time near maturity, call buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with such an offer in takes[]. Invariant tested: ConsumableUnitsLib revert must not propagate past the bundle entry point for offers that would otherwise be skipped. Scoped impact: fund freeze — entire bundle reverts due to one stale offer. Proof idea: unit test with block.timestamp = maturity - 1 second and a high-tick sell offer; assert revert with PriceGreaterThanOne().
